analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$76,343-$78,178 on May 2 (consensus ~$77,500). Up +2% in 24h. Strong April +14% monthly gain. Key resistance at $80K not yet broken. Fear & Greed Index at 26 (Fear territory, recovering from extreme low of 8 in early April).
sentiment_key_signals: BTC +2% in 24h — recovery from $75,500 lows toward $80K resistance
Fear & Greed Index: 26 — Fear territory but recovering from cycle low of 8
April ETF inflows $2.44B — strongest institutional month since October 2025
FOMC held 3.5-3.75% on April 29 (8-4 contentious vote) — no rate hike
Mining difficulty dropped 135.59T -> 131.43T (May 2) — reduces miner sell pressure
SEC approved IBIT option position limit raised to 1M contracts — bullish for liquidity
Goldman Sachs filed for Bitcoin Premium Income ETF with SEC
Consensus 2026 conference May 5-7 Miami — upcoming sentiment catalyst
Bhutan sovereign fund $287M BTC transfer — institutional activity
Ark Invest projects BTC market cap to $16T by 2030
analysis.macro_backdrop: FOMC held 3.5-3.75% on Apr 29 — most dissents (4) since 1992. No major macro releases May 2-3. Next key catalysts: ISM Services May 5, NFP May 8, CPI/PPI May 13. Next FOMC June 2026. Jobs data released May 1 (first Friday). No imminent rate catalyst.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral (0.55): BTC recovering from April lows with strong ETF institutional backing ($2.44B in April) and reduced mining sell pressure. However, F&G still at 26 (Fear), $80K resistance untested and technically risky, altcoin breadth weak. FOMC uncertainty remains (8-4 split signals internal disagreement). Mixed signals with slight bullish lean from institutional demand. Bot filter ACTIVE — confidence 0.55 >= 0.50 threshold.
